CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Nate Vandyke and Defendant Sam Flores.

Dept.505; Judge: Anne Costin; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by Natalie Y. Botelho; CSR No.9897; phone 800-826-0277; email scheduling-ca@veritext.com. (D505)
John Glugoski, Esq. representing Plaintiff Cyril Hackett.

Plaintiff Cyril Hackett was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Nate Vandyke and Defendant Sam Flores were present as observers.

Plaintiff's first amended complaint for Conversion filed September 12, 2022.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Nate Vandyke on October 13, 2022.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Sam Flores on October 20, 2022.
Default entered as to Defendant Nate Vandyke and Defendant Sam Flores on December 28, 2022.
Dismissal of Defendant Does 1 through 50 inclusive filed April 4, 2024.

Defendant Van Dyke addresses the Court and wishes to take a seat at the table. The court informs the Defendant that he is in Default and cannot address the court for this hearing however, he may observe from the gallery.

The court discloses on the record that counsel for Plaintiff, Mr. John Glugoski, Esq. is a former employer wherein the court worked as a law intern at counsel's firm from at least 15 years ago. This will not affect the court from being fair and impartial.

The court reviews the submitted documents with counsel.
Counsel orally requests the court to dismiss Defendant Robert Taylor and states that Defendant Taylor has settled in good faith.
Good cause appearing, the Court grants counsel's request and dismisses Defendant Robert Taylor from the action.

Counsel to summarize the causes of action and to offer proof as to witness testimony.
Plaintiff is called to testify. The court examines the plaintiff. Plaintiff testifies.
Plaintiff confirms that statements made, and declarations submitted are true and correct.
The court reviews the proposed judgment with counsel. Damages sought excludes the amount settled by Defendant Taylor.

Good cause appearing, the Court GRANTS judgment and rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendants as follows:
Damages $115,000.00
Interest $0
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $1,368.50
Total Judgment $116,368.50

Proposed judgment is signed by the Court.
